text: Here's what science actually says about reconsolidation memory. When we recall a memory, it can change. This is known as reconsolidation. Every time we remember, our brain rewrites the memory, making us vulnerable to errors. Research shows reconsolidation can alter emotions associated with memories. Feeling different about your past? This means our memories aren’t static. They evolve, shaping our identity in unexpected ways. Want to dive deeper into how your memories shape you? Hit subscribe!
